title: Badam Barfi (Almond Fudge)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Ghee (clarified butter) – 2 tbsp (plus extra for greasing)
Saffron – a few strands (soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk)
Edible silver leaf (varq) – optional, for garnish

instruction.title: Almond Paste
steps: Soak almonds in hot water for 30 minutes, peel, and pat dry.
Grind into a smooth paste using as little water as possible (or milk for richness).
Chef’s Tip: For a restaurant-smooth barfi, make sure the almond paste is silky and not grainy. Use a high-speed blender.

instruction.title: Sugar Syrup (Chashni)
steps: In a heavy-bottom pan, add sugar and water.
Cook until 1-string consistency (when a drop between your thumb and finger pulls a thin thread).

instruction.title: Cooking the Barfi
steps: Lower the flame, add the almond paste to the syrup.
Stir continuously on medium-low heat until the mixture thickens.
Add saffron milk, ghee, and cardamom powder.
Cook until it leaves the sides of the pan and forms a soft dough-like mass.

instruction.title: Setting
steps: Grease a tray with ghee, spread mixture evenly (about ½ inch thick).
Smooth the surface with a greased spatula.
Let it cool for 1 hour, then cut into diamond-shaped barfi pieces.

instruction.title: Plating – Restaurant Style
steps: Serve two diamond pieces per portion on a fine dessert plate.
Garnish with micro saffron strands, pistachio dust, and a drizzle of rose syrup reduction for elegance.
Accompany with a quenelle of almond milk rabri or a scoop of saffron ice cream for a fine-dining touch.

chefTips: Use premium Mamra almonds for best flavor and aroma.
Always cook on medium-low heat to avoid crystallization.
Adding a hint of rose water at the end gives a royal Mughlai essence.
For a vegan twist, substitute ghee with almond oil.
